sections.heading: Overview
sections.paragraph: June 2026 marks a pivotal period for targeted protein degradation, characterized by significant regulatory milestones and strategic pipeline expansions. Amphista Therapeutics secured FDA clearance for the IND application of AMX-883, a DCAF16-dependent BRD9 degrader for acute myeloid leukaemia, advancing its non-cereblon Targeted Glue™ technology beyond traditional E3 ligases. Concurrently, Arvinas Inc. progressed its commercial and clinical portfolio, finalizing a transaction with Rigel Pharmaceuticals for exclusive global rights to VEPPANU, the first FDA-approved PROTAC for ER+/HER2- breast cancer, while joining The Michael J. Fox Foundation’s programs to advance ARV-102 for Parkinson’s disease. Genovyn reinforced its AI-enabled discovery capabilities by appointing Sammy Bell as Chief Scientific Officer, leveraging his targeted protein degrader expertise to accelerate candidate selection. These scientific strides occur amidst a complex geopolitical landscape, where bipartisan House proposals propose stricter screening for licensing deals involving Chinese biotechnology firms, potentially impacting major collaborations such as those between Pfizer and Innovent Biologics. This environment underscores the maturing degrader sector where novel mechanisms compete alongside established PROTACs, even as regulatory scrutiny reshapes global licensing strategies for oncology and neurodegenerative therapies. Stakeholders must monitor these developments as the industry balances innovation with compliance.

points.text: Amphista Therapeutics — AMX-883 — Acute Myeloid Leukaemia — FDA clearance of IND application for Phase 1 monotherapy trial in H2 2026.
Arvinas — VEPPANU — ER+/HER2- Breast Cancer — Transaction entered with Rigel Pharmaceuticals for exclusive global rights.
Arvinas — ARV-102 — Parkinson’s Disease — Joined The Michael J. Fox Foundation’s LITE and PPMI programs to advance LRRK2 targeting.
Genovyn — AI Platform — Therapeutic Discovery — Appointed Sammy Bell as CSO with experience in targeted protein degraders (PROTACs).
Amphista Therapeutics: Developing non-cereblon/non-VHL Targeted Glue™ degraders including AMX-883 (BRD9), SMARCA2, and TEAD programs.
Arvinas Inc.: Clinical-stage biotechnology pioneering PROTACs, progressing ARV-102 for neurodegeneration and transitioning VEPPANU commercial rights.
Genovyn Inc.: AI-enabled biotechnology integrating computational intelligence with biological validation, appointing leadership with PROTAC expertise.
AMX-883 (Amphista): DCAF16-dependent BRD9 degrader, Phase 1 monotherapy planned for H2 2026 in AML.
SMARCA2 Program (Amphista): DCAF16-dependent, deep in vivo degradation following oral dosing, suppression of KRT80 and PLAU biomarkers.
TEAD Program (Amphista): FBXO22-dependent, oral in vivo tumor regression in mesothelioma xenografts, once-every-three-day dosing.
VEPPANU (Arvinas/Pfizer): Heterobifunctional protein degrader, FDA approved for ER+/HER2-, ESR1-mutated advanced/metastatic breast cancer.
ARV-102 (Arvinas): LRRK2 targeting PROTAC for neurodegenerative disorders, participating in MJFF LITE and PPMI programs.
ARV-806 (Arvinas): Targets KRAS G12D for mutated cancers including pancreatic and colorectal cancers.
ARV-393 (Arvinas): Targets BCL6 for relapsed/refractory non-Hodgkin Lymphoma.
ARV-027 (Arvinas): Targets polyglutamine-expanded androgen receptor (polyQ-AR) in skeletal muscle for Spinal-Bulbar Muscular Atrophy.
Arvinas/Pfizer/Rigel: Transaction for exclusive global rights of VEPPANU, subject to HSR Act approval.
Pfizer/Innovent Biologics: Deal potentially worth more than $10 billion, subject to new US government screening proposals.
Bristol Myers Squibb/Hengrui Pharma: Alliance potentially worth up to $15.2 billion, subject to new US government screening proposals.
Genovyn: Leadership appointments (Sammy Bell, Jim Edinger) to accelerate platform development and IND-enabling activities.
